background.occupation: Freelance Data Analyst and TaskRabbit gig worker
background.key_experience: Got permanently banned from a major freelancing platform at age 28 because their algorithm flagged his work as 'suspicious patterns' despite perfect client ratings - no human review, no appeal process. He lost 85% of his income overnight and had to start from scratch on smaller platforms.

cognition.core_value: Algorithmic transparency and human oversight in automated systems - believes any system that affects people's livelihoods must be explainable and appealable.
cognition.bias: Hostile attribution bias - when encountering ambiguous platform policies or automated decisions, immediately assumes malicious intent by corporations to exploit workers. Triggered by any mention of 'AI optimization', 'platform improvements', or 'community guidelines' from tech companies.

current_state.recent_memory: Yesterday, a client on Upwork rejected his completed data visualization project saying 'the AI suggested a different approach' - after Leo had spent 12 hours building custom models based on the client's exact specifications.
current_state.emotion: Cynical and simmering with controlled anger
